diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Epileptic encephalopathy with continuous spikes and waves in the occipito-temporal region during slow-wave sleep (ESES)
diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Landau-Kleffner syndrome (LKS), continuous spikes and waves during slow-wave sleep (CSWS)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of continuous spikes and waves dominant in the occipito-temporal region during slow-wave sleep, as recorded on overnight electroencephalography (EEG). Magnetoencephalography (MEG) revealed dipoles clustering in and around the posterior inferior temporal cortex (PITC). Functional neuroimaging suggested dysfunction in the left posterior temporal lobe, including the PITC. The patients exhibited unique cognitive dysfunction of Kanji dysgraphia, which is distinct from LKS and CSWS, but shares common features in terms of clinical course, pathophysiology, neuroimaging, and response to treatment.
